abstract: Allow people to transfer browsing history, bookmarks, reading lists, and browser extensions to or from your app using a system-provided sheet.

codeExamples.code:
let importOptions = try await browserDataImportManager.requestImport(for: importMetadata)
// Continue the import using the importOptions result.

overview:
## Overview

When someone wants to transfer data from your browser to another browser on their device, your app can present a BrowserKit *browsing data transfer sheet* that walks them through the process. In the sheet, they can choose a destination browser to which to export data. The sheet also supports importing data to your app; the person chooses the source browser from which to import data. In the sheet, the person also selects the type of data they want to transfer, including page visit history, bookmarks, reading list items, and browser extensions.

To display the framework-provided sheet, call [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager/requestExport(for:token:completionHandler:)] when the person requests exporting data from your app, and call [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager/requestImport(for:completionHandler:)] when the person requests importing data to your app.

If a person uses the sheet in a browser other than your own, the system lists your browser as available when you register for specific launch activities using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/NSUserActivityTypes]. Add the data-transfer activity strings (`BEBrowserDataExchangeExportActivity` and `BEBrowserDataExchangeImportActivity`) to your app’s target properties.

When a person chooses your app in the sheet as the source browser for a data export, the system launches your app with the [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager] class’s [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager/userActivityType-4ar5j]. When the person chooses your app as the destination browser for a data import, the system launches your app with the [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager] class’s [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager/userActivityType-35jes]. Add individual [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSUserActivity] launch handlers to fillful each type of data request.

## Initiate a browsing-data export

To export data in response to someone’s request in your app, make information available to the transfer sheet. To begin, prepare high-level information about what data is available by configuring an [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEExportMetadata] instance. Also, create a [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager] object and provide it with the window that displays your app’s view hierarchy.

After creating the export metadata, intiate the export process by calling [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager/requestExport(for:token:completionHandler:)]. As arguments to the call, pass the export metadata, `nil` for the token, and a completion handler.

The call triggers the system to present the sheet, which includes the items available for export, as defined by the metadata.

The person chooses a destination browser and items to export, and submits the sheet. The system gives your completion handler a [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEExportOptions] instance, which includes the selected data types. The sheet also indicates whether the person wants to export the data to files rather than directly to another installed browser:

The following example demonstrates a request to export bookmark data:

This call exports data for the types indicated by [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEExportOptions/dataTypes-swift.property]. After you check whether to export bookmarks, check for other data types, including browsing history, reading list items, and browser extensions, as shown below:

## Initiate a browsing-data import

Use the BrowserKit sheet to import data in response to someone’s request in your app’s user interface. Prepare high-level information about the data transfer methods you support by providing a [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEImportMetadata] instance. First create a [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager] object and pass in a reference to your app’s window scene ([doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/UIKit/UIWindowScene]).

After configuring the import metadata, initiate the import process by calling [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager/requestImport(for:completionHandler:)]. Pass the import metadata and your app’s import-submission completion handler.

The call triggers the system to present the sheet, which displays a list of available browsers to receive the data. The sheet passes your completion handler a [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEImportOptions] instance, which indicates whether the person chooses to import data from files rather than directly from another browser:

## Indicate support for and respond to data transfers

The data transfer sheet includes browsers that support transfer requests. To indicate support, use [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSUserActivity] by including the following strings in your app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/NSUserActivityTypes] target property:

When someone chooses your app in the sheet as the browser to which they want to import data or from which to export data, the system launches your app with the relevant activity, depending on the transfer type the person chose. In a SwiftUI app, implement [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/SwiftUI/View/onContinueUserActivity(_:perform:)] to respond to the activity, including:

For example:

## Provide a required token to validate the transfer

To ensure a one-to-one data transfer between the initiating and responding browsers, the system generates a unique token to track the request. Retrieve the token from the activity’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSUserActivity/userInfo] dictionary and provide it to the system at the time of the data exchange. Pass the  [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager/importTokenUserInfoKey-3bqve] user info key to indicate an import, and [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager/exportTokenUserInfoKey-1y5l1] user info key to indicate an export, as shown below:

## Receive imported data in a launch activity

When your app is the data recipient — the destination browser — in all cases, your app receives the actual browsing data within the import launch activity. In your handler, begin receiving the data as a stream by calling [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager/importBrowserData(token:)]. Pass the retrieved token as an argument and process each imported item according to its type:

Preserve the folder hierarchy of bookmarks with the [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataBookmark/parentIdentifier] property. For page visits, maintain the redirects using the redirect-related properties, such as [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataHistoryVisit/redirectSourceURL]. For extensions, consider prompting the person to install equivalent extensions from the App Store using the [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExtension/storeIdentifier] property.

## Export data in a launch activity

When someone initiates an import of browsing data from a browser into your browser, the system launches your browser with the `BEBrowserDataExchangeExportActivity` activity (the [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager] class’s [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager/userActivityType-4ar5j]). In your activity handler, raise the data transfer sheet to allow the person to select the types of data to export. Then, you export the data by calling [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataExportManager/exportBrowserData(_:)] in the same way as when your app initiates an export.

After your app streams the data, the system relaunches the browser that requested the import using the `BEBrowserDataExchangeImportActivity` (the [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager] class’s [doc://BrowserKit/documentation/BrowserKit/BEBrowserDataImportManager/userActivityType-35jes]).

items.description: A class that tests whether a device is eligible to run an alternative browser engine.
items.name: BEAvailability

title: Transferring browsing data to another browser
